text:
Once there was a man named Harold who loved to knit. He had a cozy little cottage on a hill with a porch swing that creaked melodiously in the gentle breeze. Harold spent his days creating sweaters and hats — always in vibrant, matchstick-patterned wool. It was his secret passion.

One chilly autumn, Harold entered his shop on a grey day feeling under the weather. As customers cautiously shopped among rows of needles and yarn, a young woman approached him with a hopeful smile. Her name was Emma, and she was on the verge of moving to a coastal town for college.

"Hello," she said. "I've read about your knitting. I've heard your sweaters are something else."

Harold smiled warmly. "Nothing beats hand-knitted warmth," he agreed. "How about a custom piece for you? What kind of coat were you thinking?"

She considered a moment and said, "I was thinking of a heavy coat, one that doesn't just
